summary: A.P.E.X. (Adaptive Personal Executive Xpert) is the user's custom modular AI assistant. One assistant to the user, modular systems behind the scenes. Currently building the memory foundation.
goals: Feel like a single natural assistant to the user.
Use efficient memory (catalog + resolver + small packet) instead of dumping all memory into the prompt.
Support actions/tools (calendar, email, files, hardware) as real backend jobs — later phases.
Adapt to the user via an evolving A.P.E.X. self-profile.
Stay secure: secrets isolated, permissions and approvals for risky actions.
components: Memory system (catalog, resolver, packet builder, writer)

decisions.text: Memory V1 uses Python standard library only and local JSON — no web framework, no AI provider, no dependencies.
Semantic search is faked with alias/tag/name matching for now; clean interface so embeddings can be added later.
Memory Writer is non-destructive: it logs proposed writes, never auto-edits memory.
Secrets live only in secrets/ (git-ignored) and are never referenced from memory.

open_questions: Which backend framework for Phase 2 (FastAPI vs Node/Express)?
Which AI provider powers the main brain (Claude vs GPT vs switchable)?
